'''Millinar''' was an elusive planet believed to be only a myth by [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large. The myth of Millinar was often compared to those of the [[Lost City of the Jedi]] and the [[Valley of the Jedi]].<ref name="The Dark Forces Saga">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}}</ref> The "mythic" planet was actually located in the [[Interior Region]] of the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]], a region which itself had become almost legendary by the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Description==
Millinar was located in a giant dust cloud, obscuring it from the rest of the galaxy. Its low axial tilt and good water/land surface ratio meant the planet's weather was always pleasant, and its low-lying land masses, beautiful springs, and underground streams added to an unspoiled natural beauty. The native trees possessed extensive root systems reaching down to the underground streams, and produced succulent fruits with unusual natural healing properties.<ref name="GH" />

The planet's focal point was a large, triangular monument built by an ancient [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. Its surface was decorated with elaborate paintings that seemed to move in sunlight.<ref name="GH" />

The [[Unidentified Millinar species|natives]] of the planet were a race of [[Gender/Legends|hermaphroditic]], [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] aliens, rumored to be keepers of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] techniques long lost to the galaxy due to the Emperor's purges. A legend also told of an ancient, powerful weapon hidden on the world.<ref name="GH" />
